title: Archery Targets on Straw Bales at the Llao Llao Hotel in Bariloche Rio Negro
description: Llao Llao Hotel, Río Negro: Two robust straw archery targets stand in an open, shaded clearing, serving as a focal point for outdoor recreation amidst the pristine Patagonian wilderness. The textures of the bundled stalks create a complex, earthy contrast against the crisp, circular patterns of the target faces, which remain remarkably pristine despite their exposure to the elements. Dappled sunlight filters through the overhanging canopy of coniferous trees, casting soft shadows that emphasize the dense, vertical lines of the bale construction. The composition utilizes a centered, head-on perspective to draw the viewer’s eye toward the bullseyes, highlighting the contrast between the organic, golden-brown forage and the synthetic, vivid colors of the targets. Surrounding the targets, the rugged forest floor of Bariloche transitions into the subtle slopes of the Andes, grounding this sporting activity within a distinctively Andean environment. This scene captures the intersection of structured luxury resort programming and the raw, untamed beauty of the surrounding Nahuel Huapi National Park. Archery here provides guests with an immersive opportunity to engage with the landscape in a traditional, skill-building fashion that reflects the adventurous spirit of Southern Argentina. Whether viewed as an invitation to participate or a simple aesthetic observation of resort amenities, these targets define the balance between recreation and the quiet, natural majesty of Patagonia. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
